metadata.summary: Expose review-bot capabilities as PR-comment slash commands so humans steer the bot in-band. Auto-run /describe and /review; keep /improve opt-in to avoid suggestion-patch noise.

content.title: Slash-Command Surface for PR Review Bots
content.summary: Expose review-bot capabilities as PR-comment slash commands so humans steer the bot in-band rather than through a separate dashboard. Qodo Merge / PR-Agent set the standard: `/describe` regenerates title and walkthrough, `/review` posts inline issues, `/improve` posts code-suggestion patches you can apply with one click, `/ask "why does this regex differ?"` queries the diff. Auto-trigger `/describe` plus `/review` on PR open and re-push; leave `/improve` opt-in (it's the noisy one). CodeRabbit (`@coderabbitai resolve|review`) and Sourcery follow the same surface.

why.p: Without slash commands, humans must visit a vendor dashboard or re-push a commit to re-trigger a bot — slow, friction-heavy, and outside the PR audit trail. With slash commands, every steering action is a comment in the PR, captured by GitHub history, and reproducible without leaving the review thread. Auto-running `/describe` and `/review` on open keeps the cheap-and-useful surface always-on; gating `/improve` behind explicit invocation prevents suggestion-patch noise from drowning real review.

list.item: Multi-author repos where humans want a "second pair of eyes" before requesting human review.
High-PR-volume repos (>20/day) where humans drown in description-writing.
Repos already using a slash-command-capable bot (Qodo Merge, CodeRabbit, Sourcery).
Open-source repos where contributors lack institutional context to describe risk in PR bodies.
Tiny PRs (<20 LOC) — `/review` adds more noise than it removes.
Repos with strict "no third-party app on source" policy without a self-hosted PR-Agent install.
Single-author repos where the author already writes the description — auto-describe overwrites it.
Compliance setups where bot comments must be pre-approved before posting.

section.name: command-surface
section.p: Every PR-review bot worth installing exposes the same four commands. Pinning the names makes the steering surface portable across vendors.

list.item: `/describe` — regenerate PR title and walkthrough from the diff.
`/review` — inline issues plus a structured summary (security, perf, tests, complexity).
`/improve` — post code-suggestion patches reviewers can apply one-click.
`/ask <question>` — free-form question against the diff plus repo context.

rule.statement: The bot configuration MUST set `auto_describe = true` and `auto_review = true` on PR open and on push events. `auto_improve` MUST be `false` — `/improve` MUST be opt-in by explicit comment. The `issue_comment` event MUST be wired so `/commands` posted as PR comments dispatch the matching bot action.
rule.rationale: Auto-running describe and review covers the always-useful 80%; auto-running improve produces 5-15 suggestion patches per PR and trains reviewers to ignore the bot. Qodo Merge's own usage guide and CodeRabbit production studies converged on this default.

example.code: on: pull_request: { types: [opened, reopened, ready_for_review, synchronize] } issue_comment: { types: [created] } jobs: pr_agent: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: qodo-ai/pr-agent@main env: GITHUB_ACTION_CONFIG.AUTO_DESCRIBE: "true" GITHUB_ACTION_CONFIG.AUTO_REVIEW: "true" GITHUB_ACTION_CONFIG.AUTO_IMPROVE: "false"
example.note: describe + review are always-on; improve waits for explicit `/improve`.

example.code: on: { pull_request: [opened] } # no issue_comment jobs: pr_agent: steps: - env: { GITHUB_ACTION_CONFIG.AUTO_IMPROVE: "true" } # noisy
example.note: No `issue_comment` event — slash commands silently fail. Auto-improve floods the PR with suggestion patches.

antipattern.description: Letting the bot rewrite the entire PR body on every `/describe` re-run.
antipattern.reason: Human-edited reviewer notes are erased; reviewers stop adding context because it disappears.

template.purpose: Working Qodo Merge workflow with auto-describe/auto-review.
HTML-comment-delimited block that survives bot re-runs.

see-also.link: Qodo Merge automations and usage guide.
